concept.title: GitHub Copilot: Machine Translation for Code

conbody.p: As a translator, you are likely used to CAT tools offering AutoSuggest or populating a segment with Machine Translation (MT). GitHub Copilot does exactly the same thing, but for programming languages.
It is an AI assistant built directly into VS Code. It is not activated automatically; you must install it and sign in with your GitHub account to use the Free tier.

section.title: How it works in your editor
section.p: Copilot helps you in two distinct ways:

ul.li: Ghost Text (AutoSuggest): As you type Python code, Copilot will suggest the rest of the line in faint gray text. If you like the suggestion, you press Tab to accept it.
Copilot Chat (Your Dev Support): You can open a chat sidebar in VS Code and ask questions in plain English, such as "How do I run this specific Python file?"

section.title: The Golden Rule: Context is Everything
section.p: Just like an MT engine produces better translations if it has context, Copilot gives better answers if it knows what folder you are working in.

Because you opened your my-learnings folder in VS Code, you can use the special command @workspace in the chat. This tells the AI: "Look at all the files in this specific folder before answering my question."

example.title: Example Chat Prompt
example.p: Instead of asking: "Why is my code broken?"
Ask: "@workspace I am trying to run the term extraction script, but I am getting a 'pandas not found' error. How do I fix this?"
